title: Guard/quota logic lives in a tested hooks/ package, not in the sb/ engine
context: Sub-plan B's spec §2 says 'the sb engine is untouched (B adds hooks + a monitor wiring, not engine code), except possibly tiny config keys.' But B's guard/quota/monitor logic must be unit-testable (spec §8: B is 'genuinely TDD-able, unlike A'). The v1 rabbit_guard.py is an untested repo-root script. Where the logic lives determines both testability and whether the engine boundary is respected.

options.name: hooks-as-package
options.rationale: Make hooks/ an importable package (hooks/__init__.py); put pure logic in importable functions with a thin main() shim; tests do `from hooks import sb_guard`. Hooks import sb.paths/store/channels READ-ONLY.
options.tradeoff: hooks/ becomes a second tested package alongside sb/; slight import-path care needed.

options.name: logic-in-sb-engine
options.rationale: Put guard/quota logic in new sb/guard.py + sb/quota.py modules; hooks are thin shims like sb_notify.py.
options.tradeoff: Cleanest import story, but expands the sb engine surface — contradicts the spec's 'engine untouched' and blurs the deterministic-queue-core boundary with the guard layer.

options.name: untested-root-scripts
options.rationale: Keep the v1 shape: standalone repo-root scripts, logic inline in main().
options.tradeoff: Matches v1, zero new packaging, but not unit-testable — violates spec §8's TDD requirement for B, the safety keystone.

reasoning: Testability is non-negotiable for B (the keystone for safe autonomy, PHI-030) and the spec explicitly calls B TDD-able. hooks-as-package gets full unit coverage of every tripwire/decision via synthetic payloads WITHOUT spawning real Claude Code or a model, while keeping the sb engine surface unchanged (the logic only reads engine primitives). It honors the spec's 'engine untouched' more faithfully than putting guard logic into sb/, where it would enlarge the engine the spec deliberately froze.

steelman.strongest_case: sb/ is already an installed package with a clean import story; hooks/ needs __init__.py and sys.path care to be importable in tests. Co-locating all deterministic Python in sb/ is arguably simpler and the 'engine untouched' line was about behavior, not literally the package directory.
It matches the shipped v1 pattern exactly, requires zero packaging decisions, and hooks are inherently exercised live; one could argue integration testing in D is the real proof for a hook layer.

blast_radius: New hooks/ package + its test files; two additive engine touch-points only (Layout.guard attr + guard_*/monitor_* config keys, Task 1). No change to existing engine behavior or schemas. Reversible: logic could later move to sb/ with a mechanical import change if the boundary judgment changes.
